mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede consumer spending contractions and earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls indicate a synchronized contraction in aggregate demand and labor income that amplifies recession risk beyond either signal in isolation, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of months when both RSAFS and PAYEMS decline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing activity and capacity utilization that boosts near-term earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns during periods when the federal funds rate is below 1%.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or the signal strength is statistically indistinguishable when FEDFUNDS is above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600 thousand units signals a collapsing residential construction sector that drags down construction employment, building materials demand, and mortgage credit expansion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a declining federal funds rate and a falling unemployment rate reflects a successful monetary stimulus generating real economic gains without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS declines and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate output that supports corporate revenue and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity prices track nominal economic expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating consumer price index reflecting rising inflation pressures erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL accelerates by more than 1.0% over 3 months.
